title: nauclea nemorosa
intro: Nauclea nemorosa, also known as the "Quinine Tree" or "Nauclea", is a tropical plant species native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antibacterial properties, with the bark being a rich source of the compound naucleiflorin. The medicinal properties of Nauclea nemorosa have been reported to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, including malaria. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, with the bark being boiled in water to make a tea, or dried and crushed into a powder for use in herbal remedies.
